      "text": "D E C E M B E R 2 1 , 2 0 2 3 | N E P O O L T R A N S M I S S I O N C O M M I T T E E | D O U B L E T R E E H O T E L , W E S T B O R O U G H , M A\n\nAl McBride D I R E C T O R | T R A N S M I S S I O N S E R V I C E S & R E S O U R C E Q U A L I F I C A T I O N\n\n##### Summary of Tariff Redline Design Details\n\n## Order 2023 - Improvements to Generator Interconnection Procedures and Agreements\n\n### Presentation Overview\n\n• ISO has posted the initial draft of all Tariff redlines associated with Order No. 2023 compliance \n• This presentation summarizes the key components of the compliance design and responds to several Stakeholder comments and questions \n• Focus on Large Generator Interconnection Procedures (LGIP) in Schedule 22 of the Open Access Transmission Tariff (OATT) – The Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (SGIP) in Schedule 23 of the OATT have been overhauled to mirror the LGIP \n• The pre-application process has been maintained in the SGIP – The Elective Transmission Upgrade Interconnection Procedures (ETUIP) in Schedule 25 of the OATT also closely match the LGIP \n\n### ACCEPTABLE FORM OF READINESS DEPOSITS\n\n### Commercial Readiness Deposit\n\nTariff Section Tariff Change Reason for Change\n\nLGIP\n\nCommercial Readiness Deposits that must be submitted to the System Operator under this LGIP must be delivered to the System Operator’s bank account by electronic transfer or through the provision and maintenance of a Letter of Credit in a form and from a financial institution acceptable to System Operator, as described on the System Operator’s public website. All other deposits that must be submitted to the System Operator under this LGIP must be paid in cash and delivered to the System Operator’s bank account by electronic transfer within the period specified in the respective provision. A deposit will not be considered received until it is in the System Operator’s bank account or, in the case of a Letter of Credit provided as a Commercial Readiness Deposit, the Letter of Credit is accepted by System Operator. Deposits that must be submitted to the Interconnecting Transmission Owner may be submitted in a form acceptable to the Interconnecting Transmission Owner.\n\nIncorporating Order No. 2023 proposal to allow Letter of Credit for Commercial Readiness Deposits\n\nStill require cash for study deposits\n\n### Commercial Readiness Deposit, cont.\n\nTariff Section Tariff Change Reason for Change\n\nLGIP\n\n3.4.2\n\n(vi) a Commercial Readiness Deposit equal to two times the study deposit described in Section 3.1.1.1 of this LGIP in the form of an irrevocable letter of credit in a form and from a financial institution acceptable to System Operator, as described on the System Operator’s public website, or cash where cash deposits shall be treated according to Section 3.7 of this LGIP. The letter of credit must specify the name of the Interconnection Customer to which the Commercial Readiness Deposit corresponds. This Commercial Readiness Deposit is refunded to Interconnection Customer according to Section 3.7 of this LGIP,\n\nIncorporating Order No. 2023 proposal to allow Letter of Credit for Commercial Readiness Deposits\n\n### Commercial Readiness Deposit, cont.\n\nTariff Section Tariff Change Reason for Change\n\nLGIP 5.1.1.1 & 5.1.1.2\n\n…in the form of an irrevocable letter of credit in a form and from a financial institution acceptable to System Operator, as described on the System Operator’s public website, or cash where cash deposits shall be treated according to Section 3.7 of this LGIP. The letter of credit must specify the Interconnection Request to which the Commercial Readiness Deposit corresponds.\n\nIncorporating Order No. 2023 proposal to allow Letter of Credit for Commercial Readiness Deposits\n\n### TRANSITION RELATED UPDATES\n\n### Transition Process Dates and Milestones*\n\n• “Effective Date” – ISO is proposing an Effective Date of May 31, 2024 \n• Execution of Transitional Serial Interconnection Facilities or Transitional Cluster Study Agreement is due no later than 60 Calendar Days (CD) from the Effective Date and study would commence thereafter \n• “Eligibility Date” – Consistent with Order 2023, an April 1 compliance filing date results in an Eligibility Date of May 1, 2024. This means that Interconnection Customers with a valid IR as of May 1, 2024 will be eligible to elect to proceed with a transition study or elect to withdraw from the interconnection queue without penalties. – On May 31, the ISO will issue a Transitional Serial Interconnection Facilities Study Agreement or a Transitional Cluster Study Agreement to Interconnection Customers with eligible IRs \n• Interconnection Requests that are not valid and have not been assigned Queue Position as of the Eligibility Date will be withdrawn by the ISO without further opportunity to cure any deficiencies (withdrawal penalties will not apply) – The ISO will not accept any Interconnection Requests submitted after the Eligibility Date until the first Cluster Entry Window Opens in 2025 – Ongoing study work for IRs for which an SIS is underway as of the Eligibility Date will continue through the Effective Date \n• Results of those studies will be provided for information purposes only and will not effect a project’s eligibility status with respect to the Transitional Cluster Study \n\n*All dates assume FERC acceptance of ISO-NE’s compliance proposal\n\n### Allowing for Requests to Pause On-going Studies Before Transition\n\n• Stakeholders at the TC and offline have requested that the ISO consider a process whereby ICs can request that the ISO cease the study process for projects that are actively being studied or about to begin if they have not yet started \n• The ISO has reviewed this request and identified the following applicable language in Section 2.5 of the LGIP – Parties that must perform a specific obligation under a provision of the Standard Large Generator Interconnection Procedure or Standard Large Generator Interconnection Agreement within a specified time period shall use Reasonable Efforts to complete such obligation within the applicable time period. A Party may, in the exercise of reasonable discretion and within the time period set forth by the applicable procedure or agreement, request that the relevant Party consent to a mutually agreeable alternative time schedule, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld. \n\n### Pausing On-going Studies Before Transition\n\n• Existing functionality in the Interconnection Request Tracking Tool (IRTT) allows the IC to see: – the expected completion date of an on-going study – when a study is expected to start – if it has not yet started \n• The ISO is proposing to allow an IC to voluntarily request that the ISO cease study work, i.e. “consent to a mutually agreeable alternative time schedule” \n• The option would only be provided to those IRs where the ISO has determined that the interconnection study will not be completed by the May 1, 2024 Eligibility Date under Order 2023 – Where the ISO is forecasting that the study will be completed by the eligibility deadline, the ISO will continue the study work – The ISO will also continue study work for those IRs that decline the option to have the study work paused \n\n### Pausing On-going Studies Before Transition Next Steps\n\n• ISO requests feedback from the TC on this proposal to IRTT Customer Support \n• ISO will complete review of this proposal \n• A final decision would take place in early January and, if we decide to proceed, ISO would communicate with the relevant ICs during the week of January 8 \n\n### Transition Process – Schedule of Deposits\n\n1. Any on-hand deposit associated with an IR that is eligible for the transition can be applied to transition studies. tool_input.query: ISO New England Schedule 22 LGIP "Section 3.4" "Valid Interconnection Request" "3.4.1" "3.4.2" "3.4.3" deposit site control commercial readiness

steps.content:
Based on my research of the ISO New England Open Access Transmission Tariff (OATT) Schedule 22 - Large Generator Interconnection Procedures (LGIP), effective May 3, 2025 (ER25-2149-000), and related appendices and compliance filings, here are the requirements and fees for submitting a valid interconnection application for a large generator:

## **Requirements for a Valid Interconnection Request (Section 3.4 of LGIP)**

Per the LGIP and Appendix 1 (Interconnection Request form), a valid Interconnection Request must include:

### **1. Completed Interconnection Request Form (Appendix 1)**
- **Project Information**: Proposed project name, type of request (new facility, capacity increase, market participation commencement, or service type change)
- **Type of Interconnection Service**: Must select either:
  - **Network Resource Interconnection Service (NRIS)** - energy capability only
  - **Capacity Network Resource Interconnection Service (CNRIS)** - energy and capacity capability
- **Facility Details**: 
  - Address/location (town/city, county, state)
  - Requested Point of Interconnection (POI)
  - Generating facility type and fuel type
  - Capacity data at various temperatures (maximum gross/net MW output, net MW capability at POI)
  - Requested Commercial Operation Date, Initial Synchronization Date, In-Service Date
  - Equipment configuration description (# of units, GSUs, control technologies)
  - For storage: charging capability, reactive capability, primary frequency response range

### **2. Site Control Evidence (Section 3.4.2/3.4.3)**
**For CNRIS requests**: 100% exclusive Site Control in Interconnection Customer's name must be provided.

**For NRIS requests**: Either:
- 100% exclusive Site Control in Interconnection Customer's name, **OR**
- In lieu of Site Control: 
  - A **$10,000/MW deposit** (minimum $500,000, maximum $2,000,000), refundable within the cure period per Section 3.4.3
  - A signed officer affidavit stating Site Control is unobtainable due to Regulatory Limitations
  - Documentation describing the source and effects of such Regulatory Limitations

**For modifications to existing facilities**: Certification that Site Control exists and no additional real property is needed.

### **3. Customer Information**
- Company name, ISO Customer ID#, address, representative contact details
- Authorized signature, name, title, date

### **4. Cluster Request Window Timing**
Per Order No. 2023 compliance, Interconnection Requests can **only be submitted during a Cluster Request Window**. The next window is expected **October 5, 2026 – November 19, 2026** (per ISO-NE Interconnection Process Guide).

---

## **Fees and Deposits Required**

### **1. Study Deposit (Section 3.1.1.1)**
- **Amount**: Defined in Section 3.1.1.1 of the LGIP (specific dollar amount per MW or fixed amount - the current LGIP references this section but the exact amount is in the tariff text)
- **Purpose**: Funds the Cluster Study
- **Payment**: Must be delivered to System Operator's bank account by electronic transfer (cash only for study deposits)

### **2. Commercial Readiness Deposit (Section 3.4.2(vi))**
- **Amount**: **Equal to two times (2×) the study deposit** described in Section 3.1.1.1
- **Form**: 
  - Irrevocable Letter of Credit from a financial institution on the ISO's List of Eligible Letter of Credit Issuers, **OR**
  - Cash (treated per Section 3.7 withdrawal provisions)
- **Requirements**: Must specify the Interconnection Customer name and Interconnection Request
- **Refund**: Refunded to Interconnection Customer according to Section 3.7 of the LGIP

### **3. Interconnection Facilities Study Deposit (Section 8.1 / Appendix 3)**
- **Amount**: Greater of **25% of estimated Interconnection Facilities Study cost** or **$250,000**
- **Plus**: Commercial Readiness Deposit per Section 8.1
- **Payment**: Applied toward actual study costs; difference refunded or billed
- **Monthly invoicing**: Actual costs invoiced monthly, payable within 30 calendar days

### **4. LGIA Deposit (Order No. 2023 requirement)**
- Required upon execution of the Large Generator Interconnection Agreement (post-study phase)

---

## **Key Process Changes Under Order No. 2023 Compliance**

1. **Cluster Study Process**: Replaces serial studies; requests only accepted during Cluster Request Windows
2. **Increased Financial Commitments**: Higher study deposits, Commercial Readiness Deposits (2× study deposit), and LGIA deposits
3. **Site Control Requirements**: Stricter for CNRIS (100% exclusive required); NRIS has deposit alternative
4. **Withdrawal Penalties**: Failure to meet deadlines or cure deficiencies results in withdrawal per Section 3.7
5. **Letter of Credit Options**: Available for Commercial Readiness Deposits (not for study deposits which require cash)

---

## **Sources**

1. **ISO-NE OATT Schedule 22 LGIP** (Effective 5/3/25, ER25-2149-000) - https://www.iso-ne.com/static-assets/documents/regulatory/tariff/sect_2/sch22/sch_22_lgip.pdf
   - Sections 3.1, 3.2, 3.4 (Valid Interconnection Request), 3.7 (Withdrawal), 8.1 (Facilities Study Agreement)

2. **Appendix 1 - Interconnection Request Form** - https://www.iso-ne.com/static-assets/documents/100030/oatt-schedule-22-appendix-1-large-generator-lgip-interconnection-request-with-attachments.pdf
   - Site control requirements, deposit amounts ($10,000/MW, $500k-$2M range), project data requirements

3. **Appendix 3 - Interconnection Facilities Study Agreement** - https://www.iso-ne.com/static-assets/documents/100030/oatt-schedule-22-appendix-3-large-generator-lgip-interconnection-facilities-study-agreement.pdf
   - Section 5.0: Commercial Readiness Deposit + greater of 25% estimated study cost or $250,000

4. **Order No. 2023 Compliance Presentations** (Dec 2023, Feb 2024) - https://www.iso-ne.com/static-assets/documents/100006/a06_12212023_order_2023_compliance_december_tc_summary_presentation.pdf and https://www.iso-ne.com/static-assets/documents/100008/a03b_2024_02_15_tc_order2023_redlines_update_presentation.pdf
   - Commercial Readiness Deposit = 2× study deposit (Section 3.4.2(vi))
   - Letter of Credit eligibility for Commercial Readiness Deposits
   - Cluster Request Window timing

5. **ISO-NE Interconnection Process Guide** - https://www.iso-ne.com/genrtion_resrcs/nwgen_inter/index.html
   - Next Cluster Request Window: October 5 – November 19, 2026

---

**Note**: The exact study deposit amount per MW is specified in Section 3.1.1.1 of the current LGIP tariff text. Developers should consult the most recent posted version on the ISO-NE website and the Interconnection Request Tracking Tool (IRTT) for current values and any updates.
